title: rosa × dumetorum subsp. obtusifolia
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subsp. obtusifolia, also known as Rosa obtusifolia, is a hybrid of wild rose species native to the Caucasus region, characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Rosa obtusifolia include relief from digestive issues, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Rosa obtusifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall well-being and reduce symptoms of various health conditions.
